name: Aurangabad
description: Aurangabad is a magnificent destination in Maharashtra, India. Known for its remarkable blend of history, culture, and natural beauty. Visitors flock here to experience its unique atmosphere, vibrant local markets, and iconic landmarks. Whether you are seeking spiritual peace, thrilling adventures, or simply a relaxing holiday, Aurangabad offers a memorable experience that captures the true essence of Indian heritage. The city's charm lies in its timeless appeal and warm hospitality.
history: Aurangabad boasts a rich history spanning several centuries, serving as a prominent center for trade, culture, and power under various dynasties.

topAttractions.name: Ajanta Caves (UNESCO) — Day Trip
topAttractions.description: 30 Buddhist cave monasteries and temples (1st BCE – 5th CE) carved into horseshoe-shaped cliffs 107km from Aurangabad. Caves 1 and 2 have the finest painted murals in the world — detailed court scenes, Jataka tales, and the life of the Buddha painted with extraordinary skill 2,000 years ago. Entry: ₹40 (Indian), ₹600 (foreign). Open Tue-Sun.

topAttractions.name: Ellora Caves (UNESCO) — Day Trip
topAttractions.description: 34 rock-cut temples (Buddhist, Hindu, Jain, 6th-11th century CE) covering 2km of cliff face 30km from Aurangabad. The Kailasa Temple (Cave 16) — dedicated to Shiva, carved from a single basalt rock by 7,000 laborers over 150 years — is the largest single rock-cut structure in the world (83m × 47m × 33m). Entry: ₹40 (Indian), ₹600 (foreign).

topAttractions.name: Bibi Ka Maqbara
topAttractions.description: A 1679 CE Mughal tomb built by Aurangzeb's son Azam Shah for his mother Dilras Banu Begum — called the "Taj of the Deccan" or "Mini Taj" for its white marble and resemblance to the Taj Mahal. The marble was imported from Rajasthan, the rest in lime plaster. Entry: ₹25 (Indian), ₹300 (foreign).

topAttractions.name: Daulatabad Fort (Deogiri)
topAttractions.description: A virtually impregnable 13th-century fort on a 200m high volcanic cone 15km from Aurangabad. Built by the Yadava dynasty, later strengthened by the Delhi Sultanate and Muhammad bin Tughluq (who briefly moved the entire Delhi capital here in 1327). The spiral interior moat and the dark tunnel (requiring a candle) are extraordinary. Entry: ₹25 (Indian), ₹300 (foreign).

topAttractions.name: Aurangabad Caves
topAttractions.description: A group of 12 Buddhist rock-cut caves (6th-8th CE) in two groups — the best preserved in Maharashtra apart from Ajanta-Ellora. Cave 3 has a remarkable 10-arm standing Bodhisattva and Cave 7 has elaborate carvings. Less visited than the famous UNESCO sites, making for a peaceful visit. Entry: ₹25 (Indian).

topAttractions.name: Panchakki (Water Mill)
topAttractions.description: A 17th-century underground water channel system that drove a massive millstone to grind grain for the Dargah of Hazrat Baba Shah Musafir Chishti. The waterfall created by the channel and the scenic garden complex are beautiful. The Dargah itself is an active Sufi shrine.

culture: The culture of Aurangabad is a vibrant tapestry of traditional arts, festivals, and culinary delights, deeply influenced by its historical roots.
travelTips: Book accommodations in advance during peak season
Try the local street food but ensure it is hot and fresh
Use authorized taxis or rideshare apps
Respect local customs and dress modestly at religious sites

itineraries.name: Highlights Tour
activities: Day 1: Arrival, City Center Attractions, Evening Market
Day 2: Historical Sites, Local Food Tasting, Departure
